features.summary: Meta reported Q4 2021 revenue of $33.7 billion (up 20% YoY) and full-year revenue of $118 billion (up 37% YoY), marking the first time exceeding $100 billion annually. Family of Apps ad revenue was $32.6 billion (up 20% YoY) while Reality Labs generated $877 million. The company faces headwinds from Apple's iOS changes, competition from TikTok, and the transition to short-form video content like Reels, which currently monetizes at lower rates than Feed and Stories. Meta added over 3,700 employees in Q4, ending with 71,900 total employees.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q1 2022 revenue of $27-29 billion (3-11% YoY growth), significantly slower than recent quarters due to continued headwinds from iOS changes, competition, and the Reels transition. Total expenses are projected at $90-95 billion for 2022, with capital expenditures of $29-34 billion focused on AI/ML infrastructure. The company outlined seven investment priorities for 2022: Reels, community messaging, commerce, ads, privacy, AI, and the metaverse, with Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase meaningfully in 2022.
features.risks: Apple's iOS changes are estimated to create a $10 billion revenue headwind in 2022, particularly impacting small business advertisers who rely on personalized targeting. Increased competition from TikTok is pressuring user growth, especially among younger demographics. The transition to short-form video creates near-term monetization challenges as Reels generates lower ad revenue per impression than traditional Feed content. Additional regulatory changes in Europe and macroeconomic factors including supply chain disruptions and inflation are creating further advertiser budget pressures.
features.key_drivers: Reels is driving the fastest content format growth and is the biggest contributor to engagement growth on Instagram, though monetization remains below Feed rates. The company is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and privacy-enhancing technologies to rebuild ad targeting capabilities with less data. Reality Labs showed strong Quest 2 sales during the holiday season with over $1 billion spent on Quest store content. Business messaging continues growing with over 1 billion users connecting with businesses weekly across Meta's messaging services, representing a significant long-term opportunity.
